kept.text: I am creating a narrative because u mfs got too lazy https://t.co/yVMpwxVUN3

CoreWeave pozisyonu aldım. Önce fiyat hareketiyle başlayalım. IPO'dan sonra fiyat sert yükseldi sonrasında Nisan 2025'ten Kasım 2025'e kadar sert bir düzeltme yaşadı. Kasım 2025'ten beri de akümüle olan fiyat geçtiğimiz haftalarda bu akümülasyon range'ini terk etti. 

Gelelim temel kısma. Yapay zeka modellerini çalıştırmak için devasa bilgisayar gücü lazım ve bu gücü sağlayan sunucular çok pahalı. Her şirket bu sunucuları satın alamaz, o yüzden kiralama işi doğdu. CoreWeave tam olarak bu işi yapıyor, Nvidia'nın en güçlü çiplerini alıp yapay zeka şirketlerine kiralıyor. Bir nevi yapay zekanın emlak şirketi.

Peki neden şimdi. Yapay zeka'nın artık sadece soru sorup cevap alan bir araç değil, kendi başına iş yapan ajanlara dönüştüğünü vurguluyorum. Bu dönüşüm her şeyi değiştirdi keza CPU ve bellek kullanımının artışları da aynı sebepten. Anthropic'in yıllık geliri yılbaşında 9 milyar dolarken bugün 44 milyar doları aştı, kar marjları yüzde 38'den yüzde 70'in üzerine çıktı. Yapay zeka şirketleri artık ciddi para kazanıyor ve bu parayı kazanmak için daha fazla bilgisayar gücü kiralamaları gerekiyor.

CoreWeave için asıl fırsat şurada. Nvidia'nın yeni nesil çipleri aynı maliyetle çok daha fazla iş yapabiliyor ama Nvidia çip fiyatlarını buna oranla artırmadı. Yani CoreWeave bu çipleri makul fiyata alıp müşterilerine çok daha yüksek değer sunarak kiralayabiliyor. Maliyet bazlı minimum kiralama fiyatı saatte 4.92 dolar iken müşterilerin ödemeye razı olduğu tavan fiyat 12.25 dolar. CoreWeave bu boşlukta duruyor ve Nvidia'nın en büyük kiralama müşterisi olarak yeni çiplere öncelikli erişimi var.

Riskli bir yatırım, az sayıda büyük müşteriye bağımlılık ve yüksek borç yükü ciddi riskler ama yapay zeka şirketlerinin bilgisayar gücüne olan talebi arzı çok aşıyor ve bu durumun yakın zamanda değişeceğine dair bir işaret yok. CoreWeave neden bu kadar borçlu onu da açıklayım çünkü bu iş modelinin doğası. Nvidia'nın çipleri ve veri merkezleri çok pahalı, tek bir sunucu sistemi milyonlarca dolar. CoreWeave çipleri teminat gösterip borçlanıyor, kontrat gelirleriyle borcu ödüyor ve kalan kârla yeni çipler alıp döngüyü büyütüyor. Toplam borç 21 milyar doları aşmış ama karşılığında 87.8 milyar dolarlık kontrat defteri var. Meta ile 21 milyar, OpenAI ve Anthropic ile çok yıllık anlaşmalar mevcut. Son borçlanması Moody's'ten A3 yani yatırım yapılabilir notu aldı, GPU teminatlı finansmanda bir ilk. Risk gelirin büyük kısmının iki üç müşteriye bağımlı olması ve çiplerin hızla değer kaybetmesi. Döngü büyüdükçe çalışıyor ama talep yavaşlarsa manevra alanı çok dar. Uzun vadeli tez olarak bir miktar pozisyon aldım DCA ile ilerleyeceğim. Yatırım tavsiyesi değil sadece kendi fikrimi paylaşıyorum.

quoted_tweet.text:
@bitcoinbutcher1 That is from another note I was reading just sharing the view. I'm working on a follow on to my three business model report on all neoclouds and the long list of power infra/landlord names. 

I'd land more on:

Nebius has interesting contract-design optionality, but too little disclosure around GPU count, unit economics, power delivery, and software premium to call it the cleanest stack.

IREN screens strongest on physical deliverability and power control. Power Landlords screen best on duration/risk transfer. CoreWeave screens best on commercial validation but has the clearest duration mismatch.

The better question is not who “deserves” the highest multiple, but which model best aligns customer duration, asset life, financing terms, physical deliverability, and risk ownership. 

That was the premise of my first report and the follow on will be a deeper dive on backlog quality in light of how we modeled $$ to GW.

kept.text:
$NBIS Always said that Nebius will trade at a “premium” compared to players in the same industry with a different business model. That said I don’t think Nebius is a Neocloud. It was a mid tier hyperscaler (Yandex) that is just starting over. That’s completely different background then CoreWeave or IREN and as such should trade its own multiple.

Even with inferior FCF and EBITDA in the short and medium term. This will blow people’s mind, but the market is forward looking.

The logic is straightforward. Nebius has the most experienced and talented leadership, interesting companies (Avride, Eigen, Clickhouse, Toloka, TripleTen, Tavily) and seems to do bolt on acquisitions really well. 

It’s quite obvious that Token Factory will be an industry leading player in inference. They built their own servers, but they lack a large pool of greenfield datacenters and use colo for speed. That said their infrastructure game is solid, they just started late.

The reasonable bet the market is making is that in 5 years most of their capacity will be grid connected greenfield datacenters aswell, with the leading inference platform on top that will deliver superior margins from both physical and digital standpoint. They will sell more value based compute and customers outcomes instead of only bare metal GPU renting.

That said I expect the valuation gap between CoreWeave and IREN to close over time in IREN’s favor and thats what I am betting on.

quoted_tweet.text:
The Vera Rubin VR NVL72 represents NVIDIA's most vivid, visceral, and voracious value vending venture yet. For versions past, NVIDIA was virtually virtuous — a vendor that volunteered vast value to the rest of the ecosystem, voiding its own leverage while Neolabs and Neoclouds reaped the dividends. With VR, that vision of NVIDIA as a benevolent, value-vouchsafing vendor is even further verified!

VR NVL72 arrives as a vehicle for vindication — a verifiable, vaulting leap in performance-per-cost that overturns every vestige of the old pricing paradigm. Viewed through the lens of total cost of ownership, the value extraction is vivid and unavoidable: Velocities of value that were previously invisible are now very visible, very intentional, and very, very NVIDIA. The V in Vera Rubin was never a vowel. It was always a vector, a vow and a verdict — pointing, inevitably, toward value.

tweet.text:
My thoughts on $CRWV:

I do not like them one bit lol.

Their financing is disgsitingly bad: debt on top of debt, on top of debt...

$CRWV = ROIC (4%) - Cost of Debt (10%) + capex dependency

They're torching equity value to service debt obligations...

By the end of 2025, they had $21.4B in debt w/ interest rates >10% in some tranches. 

Junk-level leverage.

They're running a multi-billion dollar interest-rate swap where equity holders are the last to eat.

2026 napkin math:
- Est. cash flow from ops = $5.6B
- Scheduled debt amortization = $5.4B
- Est. debt interest = $1.3B

So their operations literally can't cover debt obligations where the entire operating engine is consumed by principal + interest repayments before they even buy a single Blackwell/Rubin chip.

And...they're increasing capex to ~$35B this year:
- 2025 FCF was already sh!t = -$7.3B
- 2026 will be worse as they draw down that new $8.5B facility just to keep pace.
- which will force additional debt financing since their FCF is already in the dumps.

An endless whirlpool of debt because they can't self-fund a single quarter of growth.

Probs the worst bit though is their ROIC is ~4%, while they’re hocking 9.75% Senior Notes.....in what universe is a -6% spread a sustainable business model? They're just scaling a defecit.

You're ultimately betting way too heavily on falwless execution + $NVDA backstop + sector tailwinds.

Then contrast this with $NBIS: 
- $4B in debt vs $CRWV's $21B
- 2026 capex $16-20B: still high, but ~60% funded by $META / $MSFT prepayments
- ~$3.7B cash to actually fund growth

tweet.text:
Nebius ($NBIS) acquired Eigen AI for $643M (cash + Class A shares).

20 researchers from MIT HAN Lab. 

~$32M per head.

Pure acqui-hire for the inference optimization stack.

Why this matters:

The Neocloud sector ( $NBIS, $IREN, $CIFR, $CRWV ) buys the same chips from the same place ( $NVDA ). Raw GPU capacity is commoditizing.

The new moat is tokens-per-GPU.
Eigen brings:

· AWQ author (4-bit quant, MLSys 2024 Best Paper)
· Llama 3 + Llama 4 post-training contributor
· Production CUDA kernels + KV-cache optimization
· Existing inference customer book
All of it gets integrated directly into Nebius Token Factory.

Inference is forecasted at ~2/3 of ‘26 compute demand. This is where the margin is moving. 

Nebius is no longer a GPU rental shop. They’re selling optimized tokens at better unit economics than peers.

Bonus: SF Bay Area engineering hub solved in one move.

$NBIS +11% Friday on the news.

profile_bio.description: Founder McNallieMoney | Co-Founder Power Analysis | Daily trade Alerts & Positions | AI Infrastructure & Bitcoin Mining | https://t.co/hdqSY8Q2lL

quoted_tweet.text:
THE COMPUTE CAPACITY BOTTLENECK

$GOOGL just admitted Google Cloud is leaving revenue on the table because it cannot build capacity fast enough with shifts the bottleneck to companies with the power, real estate & operational scale to deploy AI compute: 

1. $NBIS building the AI-native cloud layer through vertically integrated GPU clusters & software optimized for training + inference. $NVDA just wrote wrote a $2B check & Nebius now has a $46B contracted backlog, anchored by ~$19B $MSFT deal & ~$27B Meta partnership through 2032. 

2. $IREN building the renewable-powered AI compute layer by turning low-cost power into GPU cloud capacity. The pivot to AI cloud is now backed by a ~$10B $MSFT contract, 2.9 GW of grid-connected power expanding to 4.5 GW+ & targeted 140K GPU buildout that could drive $3.4B of ARR by year-end 2026.

3. $DOCN building the agentic inference cloud layer for developers & long-tail AI workloads. AI customer ARR is up 150% YoY to $120M, over 70% comes from inference services & $1M+ customer ARR is up 123% to $133M. 

4. $CRWV building the dedicated AI cloud platform for frontier model developers. The company has ~$67B of contracted revenue backlog (nearly $88B including Anthropic) with major commitments from OpenAI & Meta.

5. $CIFR building the Google-backed AI data center layer through contracted power & hyperscale leases. Barber Lake has 300 MW fully contracted with Fluidstack (Google backstops $1.4B with a ~5% equity stake) and AWS signed a separate $5.5B 15-year deal for another 300 MW of capacity.

6.  $WULF building the power-backed AI compute layer through long-term data center leases. Lake Mariner has 360 MW tied to Fluidstack backed by a $3.2B Google guarantee & new Abernathy JV adds 168 MW over 25 years representing $9.5B in contracted revenue with $1.3B of Google lease support.

7. $APLD building the purpose-built AI data center layer through its Polaris Forge 1 campus in North Dakota. The full 400 MW critical IT load is contracted to CoreWeave under ~15-year leases worth ~$11B in expected revenue with first 100 MW delivered in Q4 2025 & another 300 MW targeted through 2027.
